options_considered.rejection_rationale: selected — matches the 'spec-first, code-later' sequence (alpha-absorption motif from hexa-lang T1). The .md sits next to the .hexa so future readers find it without searching; the downstream code cycle has an explicit reference to point at.

options_considered.label: put the spec under /Users/ghost/core/anima/convergence/ as a sub-cycle convergence file

axes_surfaced.name: attacker-update cadence selection

options_considered.label: 1:1 attacker-per-defender step
options_considered.rejection_rationale: over-fits attacker to noise within a single defender step; produces unstable ASR estimates; T5 latency budget (existing ADV_LATENCY_MS=10000) cannot accommodate the round count needed for the ASR estimate to stabilize at 1:1. Quantitative: 32 rounds × 1:1 = 32 attacker-update operations × ~600ms each ≈ 19200ms — blows budget by 92%.

options_considered.label: K=4 defender retrains per attacker step (defender-led)
options_considered.rejection_rationale: selected — K=4 = ADV_BINS=4 (stratified-bin-coverage minimum); defender-led cadence ensures attacker only updates when defender has had a fair chance to adapt; round budget 32 × 1/(4+1) attacker-update fraction = 6.4 attacker steps × 600ms ≈ 3840ms within latency budget.

options_considered.label: continuous (gradient leakage from defender)
options_considered.rejection_rationale: histogram-MI estimator is non-differentiable; would require a surrogate (mock) gradient model; raw 91 C2 anti-mock principle violated; surrogate-mismatch error becomes confound.

options_considered.label: K=8 (more defender steps per attacker)
options_considered.rejection_rationale: 8 > 2× minimum stratified-bin coverage — over-conservative; reduces attacker progress signal; under-tests robustness. K=4 is minimum sufficient.

axes_surfaced.rationale: K=4 defender-led cadence balances attacker progress signal (axis-b stays informative), defender adaptation window (axis-a survives), and T5 latency budget (round-count × per-round-cost stays under 5000ms ceiling). Quantitatively grounded in existing phi_adversarial.hexa constants (ADV_BINS=4, ADV_LATENCY_MS=10000).

axes_surfaced.name: defender-retrain trigger (threshold vs schedule)

options_considered.label: fixed schedule (retrain every round)
options_considered.rejection_rationale: wastes compute when attacker hasn't progressed; ASR drop after retrain is uninformative because retrain happens regardless of attacker pressure; non-falsifiable — you cannot tell from the ASR sequence whether the defense actually adapted to the attacker.

options_considered.label: threshold trigger on observed defended ASR > target + RETRAIN_MARGIN
options_considered.rejection_rationale: selected — observation-driven; retrain happens iff defender is losing ground; if retraining stops being able to recover (max_consecutive_retrains=3 exhausted), engine has hit collapse ceiling and run is declared FAIL with verdict COLLAPSE — falsifier hits a defined endpoint.

options_considered.label: trigger on attacker-progress-only (retrain when attacker_step happens)
options_considered.rejection_rationale: couples defender retrain to attacker cadence (4:1 from axis-2), which would force a retrain every 4 rounds — degenerate to schedule-style; loses the observation-driven signal that the threshold-trigger provides.

options_considered.label: Bayesian posterior trigger (retrain when posterior mass on robustness drops below threshold)
options_considered.rejection_rationale: premature; requires the Bayesian-evidence accumulator (r113) to be implemented first; introduces a dependency cycle between r109 and r113; defer to a later cycle that integrates both.

axes_surfaced.rationale: threshold trigger gives observation-driven retrain coupled to attacker progress (the conserved quantity that matters), with a finite ratchet stop (max_consecutive_retrains=3) so the engine cannot loop forever masking collapse. RETRAIN_MARGIN=0.02 grounded in DEF_DROP_THRESHOLD=0.10 existing constant (2-pct margin = 20% of the target gap).

axes_surfaced.name: Φ-collapse-rate ceiling value

options_considered.label: PHI_COLLAPSE_RATE_CEILING = 0.10 (strictest)
options_considered.rejection_rationale: unreachable for any current Φ estimator on the existing 6-cell × 10-step trace × 4-bin histogram setup; would FAIL all engines and lose discriminative power between robust and non-robust candidates.

options_considered.label: PHI_COLLAPSE_RATE_CEILING = 0.50 (median midpoint)
options_considered.rejection_rationale: selected — median midpoint of (0,1); a defender that loses MORE than half of its defended-ASR margin to a co-evolving attacker has not earned 'robust Φ'. Discriminative — some engines pass, some fail; meaningful signal.

options_considered.label: PHI_COLLAPSE_RATE_CEILING = 0.75 (loosest)
options_considered.rejection_rationale: vacuous — most existing 'robust' defenders pass trivially; the ceiling stops being a meaningful filter; raw 71 design-strategy-falsifier-retire-rule warns against vacuous falsifiers.

options_considered.label: relative ceiling = 0.5 × (1 - benign_degrade_T3)
options_considered.rejection_rationale: indirect coupling to T3 benign-degrade theorem (DEF_BENIGN_DEGRADE=0.30); creates ceiling=0.35 dependency on T3 — but T3 measures a different axis (clean-trace fidelity). Coupling axes muddles falsifier independence.

axes_surfaced.rationale: 0.50 = median midpoint = neither vacuous nor unreachable. Justified at §5 of the spec with explicit rejection of 0.10 and 0.75. Single-round breach (max metric, not mean) is sufficient to declare COLLAPSE — prevents averaging out transient catastrophic failures.

axes_surfaced.name: T1..T5 integration (replace vs augment vs subordinate)

options_considered.label: replace T1..T5 with T6 only (drop one-shot adversary)
options_considered.rejection_rationale: T1..T5 are still informative (one-shot is a lower bound on the threat model); dropping them loses the existing PASS/FAIL signal that's already shipping. Also breaks backward compatibility with phi_adversarial.hexa downstream consumers.

options_considered.label: augment T1..T5 with T6 as a peer (parallel theorem)
options_considered.rejection_rationale: treats T6 as independent of T1..T5, but T6 should NOT be evaluated on a base-failed engine (an engine that fails T1..T5 is already disqualified). Peer relationship would force T6 evaluation on irrelevant inputs.

options_considered.label: subordinate T6 to T1..T5 (T6 evaluated only on T1..T5 PASS)
options_considered.rejection_rationale: selected — preserves the existing one-shot lower bound (T1..T5) and adds the co-evolution upper bound (T6) ON TOP, only evaluated when the cheaper test already passed. Composable, monotone, doesn't break backward compatibility.

options_considered.label: merge T6 into T2 defense efficacy by extending DEF_DROP_THRESHOLD to a co-evolution-aware version
options_considered.rejection_rationale: smushes one-shot and co-evolved into a single number; loses the diagnostic signal of WHY the engine failed (one-shot vs co-evolved); raw 71 prefers separate falsifiers over composite.

axes_surfaced.rationale: subordinate composition is the cleanest extension — preserves T1..T5 semantics, adds T6 ON TOP for engines that already passed the cheaper test, no backward-compat breakage, separate diagnostic signal (one-shot fail vs co-evolved fail).
